title: साधन पञ्चकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री साधन पञ्चकम् ॥

वेदो नित्यमधीयतां तदुदितं कर्म स्वनुष्ठीयतां
तेनेशस्य विधीयतामपचितिः काम्ये मतिस्त्यज्यताम्।
पापौघः परिधूयतां भवसुखे दोषोऽनुसन्धीयतां
आत्मेच्छा व्यवसीयतां निजगृहात्तूर्णं विनिर्गम्यताम्॥१॥

सङ्गः सत्सु विधीयतां भगवतो भक्तिर्दृढाऽऽधीयतां
शान्त्यादिः परिचीयतां दृढतरं कर्माशु सन्त्यज्यताम्।
सद्विद्वानुपसृप्यतां प्रतिदिनं तत्पादुका सेव्यतां
ब्रह्मैकाक्षरमर्थ्यतां श्रुतिशिरोवाक्यं समाकर्ण्यताम्॥२॥

वाक्यार्थश्च विचार्यतां श्रुतिशिरःपक्षः समाश्रीयतां
दुस्तर्कात्सुविरम्यतां श्रुतिमतस्तर्कोऽनुसन्धीयताम्।
ब्रह्मास्मीति विभाव्यतामहरहर्गर्वः परित्यज्यतां
देहेऽहंमतिरुज्झ्यतां बुधजनैर्वादः परित्यज्यताम्॥३॥

क्षुद्व्याधिश्च चिकित्स्यतां प्रतिदिनं भिक्षौषधं भुज्यतां
स्वाद्वन्नं न तु याच्यतां विधिवशात्प्राप्तेन सन्तुष्यताम्।
शीतोष्णादि विषह्यतां न तु वृथा वाक्यं समुच्चार्यतां
औदासीन्यमभीप्स्यतां जनकृपानैष्ठुर्यमुत्सृज्यताम्॥४॥

एकान्ते सुखमास्यतां परतरे चेतः समाधीयतां
पूर्णात्मा सुसमीक्ष्यतां जगदिदं तद्बाधितं दृश्यताम्।
प्राक्कर्म प्रविलाप्यतां चितिबलान्नाप्युत्तरैः श्लिष्यतां
प्रारब्धं त्विह भुज्यतामथ परब्रह्मात्मना स्थीयताम्॥५॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितं साधनपञ्चकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sadhana Panchakam ||

Vedo Nityamadheeyataam Taduditam Karma Swanushtheeyataam
Teneshsya Vidheeyataamapachitih Kaamye Matistyajyataam.
Paapaughah Paridhooyataam Bhavasukhe Dosho'nusandheeyataam
Atmechchhaa Vyavaseeyataam Nijagrihaattoornam Vinirgamyataam.||1||

Sangah Satsu Vidheeyataam Bhagavato Bhaktirdridhaa'dhee​yataam
Shaantyaadih Paricheeyataam Dridhataram Karmaashu Santyajyataam.
Sadvidvaanupasripyataam Pratidinam Tatpaadukaa Sevyataam
Brahmaikaa​ksharam​arthyataam Shrutishirovakyam Samaakarn​yataam.||2||

Vaakyaarthashcha Vichaaryataam Shrutishirah​pakshah Samaashreeyataam
Dustarkaatsuviramyataam Shrutimatastark o'nusandheeyataam.
Brahmaasmeeti Vibhaavyataam​aharahargarvah Parityajyataam
Dehe'ham​matirujjhyataam Budhajanairvaa​dah Parityajyataam.||3||

Kshuddvyaadhishcha Chikitsyataam Pratidinam Bhikshaushadham Bhujyataam
Svaadvannam Na Tu Yaachyataam Vidhivashaat​praaptena Santushyataam.
Sheetoshnaadi Vishahyataam Na Tu Vrithaa Vaakyam Samuchch​aaryataam
Audaaseenyam​abheepsyataam Janakripaan​aishthuryam​utsrijyataam.||4||

Ekaante Sukhamaasyataam Paratare Chetah Samaadhee​yataam
Poornaa​tmaa Susameekshyataam Jagadidam Tadbaadhitam Drishyataam.
Praakkarma Pravilaapyataam Chitibalaan​naapyuttaraih Shlishyataam
Praarabdham Tviha Bhujyataam​atha Parabrahmaa​tmanaa Stheeyataam.||5||

|| Iti Shrimachhankaraacharyavirachitam Saadhanapanchakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) वेद का नित्य अध्ययन करो, उसमें कहे कर्म का अनुष्ठान करो, उससे ईश्वर की पूजा करो, काम्य कर्मों में मति त्यागो। पाप-समूह को धो डालो, भव-सुख में दोष देखो, आत्मेच्छा (मोक्ष की इच्छा) दृढ़ करो, अपने घर से शीघ्र निकलो।

(2) सत्संग करो, भगवान की दृढ़ भक्ति करो, शान्ति आदि गुणों को अपनाओ, कर्म दृढ़तापूर्वक शीघ्र त्यागो। सद्विद्वान के पास जाओ, प्रतिदिन उनकी पादुकाओं की सेवा करो, एकाक्षर ब्रह्म (ॐ) का अर्थ जानो, श्रुतिशिरो (उपनिषद्) वाक्य सुनो।

(3) वाक्यार्थ का विचार करो, श्रुति-शिर (उपनिषद्) के पक्ष का आश्रय लो, कुतर्क से विरत हो, श्रुति के अनुसार तर्क करो। 'ब्रह्मास्मि' (मैं ब्रह्म हूँ) का अनुभव करो, प्रतिदिन गर्व त्यागो, देह में अहंभाव छोड़ो, विद्वानों से व्यर्थ वाद-विवाद त्यागो।

(4) क्षुधा-रोग की चिकित्सा करो (भूख लगे तो खाओ), प्रतिदिन भिक्षा-औषध (भिक्षान्न) भोगो, स्वादिष्ट अन्न की याचना मत करो, विधि से जो मिले उससे सन्तुष्ट रहो। शीत-उष्ण आदि सहो, व्यर्थ वचन मत बोलो, उदासीनता अपनाओ, जनकृपा की निष्ठुरता (निर्लिप्तता) अपनाओ।

(5) एकान्त में सुखपूर्वक बैठो, परतत्त्व में चित्त को समाधिस्थ करो, पूर्णात्मा का सम्यक् दर्शन करो, इस जगत को उससे बाधित (मिथ्या) देखो। प्राक्-कर्म (संचित) को चित्-बल से विलीन करो, उत्तर (आगामी) कर्मों से न लिपटो, प्रारब्ध को यहाँ भोगो, और फिर परब्रह्मात्मा रूप में स्थित हो जाओ।
benefits: Shankaracharya's complete roadmap for spiritual practice in just five verses
Forty practical instructions covering Karma, Bhakti, Jnana, and Vairagya
Step-by-step guide from Vedic study to final Brahman-realization
Teaches contentment, dispassion, and proper relationship with Guru
Culminates in the supreme instruction: Parabrahmatmana Sthiyatam (Abide as Brahman)
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Sadhana Panchakam (साधन पञ्चकम्) is a set of five devotional verses revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Adi Shankaracharya. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to forty practical instructions covering Karma, Bhakti, Jnana, and Vairagya as well as step-by-step guide from Vedic study to final Brahman-realization. Additionally, it is known to shankaracharya' complete roadmap for spiritual practice in just five verses. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Guru Purnima. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sadhana Panchakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Sadhana Panchakam by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Complete spiritual roadmap in 5 verses - 40 instructions for liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sadhana Panchakam and why is it considered Adi Shankaracharya's spiritual testament?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sadhana Panchakam (साधन पञ्चकम्), also called Upadesha Panchakam, is a famously compact yet complete five-verse spiritual manual composed by Sri Adi Shankaracharya, traditionally regarded as the final instruction (last upadesha) given by the Acharya before his mahasamadhi. In just five Shardulavikridita-meter verses — eight quarters per verse, summing to forty terse instructions — Shankara compresses the entire sadhana of Advaita Vedanta from the very beginning (Vedo Nityam Adheeyataam — let the Veda be daily studied) to the absolute summit (Parabrahmatmana Stheeyataam — abide as Para-Brahman itself). It is the most concentrated step-by-step liberation manual in all of classical Hindu literature. (Source: Stotra Sahitya of Adi Shankaracharya; Advaita parampara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous opening line Vedo Nityam Adheeyataam?
seoFaqs.answer: The very first instruction of the Sadhana Panchakam — Vedo Nityam Adheeyataam — means: let the Veda be studied daily, without break. This single line establishes the foundation of all spiritual life in Sanatana Dharma — direct, daily, regular contact with revealed scripture. Shankara then immediately specifies what flows from this study: Tad-uditam karma svanushtheeyataam — let the karma enjoined by the Veda be properly performed; Tena ishasya vidheeyatam-apachitih — by that karma let worship of Ishvara be done; Kamye matistyajyataam — let the desire for the fruits be abandoned; Papaughah paridhooyataam — let the flood of sins be washed away. The opening verse alone is a complete karma-yoga prescription. (Source: Sadhana Panchakam verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the five themes (or stages) of the Sadhana Panchakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The five verses correspond to the classical five-stage progression of Advaita sadhana: (1) Verse 1 — Karma Yoga: Vedic study, performance of duty, abandonment of selfish desire, washing away of sins, and renunciation of home for spiritual quest; (2) Verse 2 — Bhakti and Sat-sanga: company of the wise, devotion to God, cultivation of shanti and other gunas, surrender to a Sadguru, study of Mahavakyas; (3) Verse 3 — Jnana-vichara: contemplation of scriptural meaning, abandonment of false logic, meditation on Brahmasmi, abandonment of ego; (4) Verse 4 — Vairagya and Tapas: contentment with bhiksha food, endurance of heat-cold, silence, indifference; (5) Verse 5 — Samadhi and Liberation: solitude, meditation on the Full Self, dissolution of past karma, abiding as Brahman. (Source: Sadhana Panchakam verses 1-5.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the culminating instruction Parabrahmatmana Stheeyataam?
seoFaqs.answer: The very last words of the Sadhana Panchakam — Parabrahmatmana Stheeyataam — mean: abide as Para-Brahman itself. After forty graduated instructions covering Veda study, karma, bhakti, sat-sanga, vichara, vairagya and meditation, Shankara concludes with the supreme instruction: simply be that which you are — the unconditioned, infinite, formless Brahman. The verb stheeyataam (let one abide) is in the passive voice, suggesting that this is not a doing but an effortless settling — when all the previous sadhanas are complete, abidance happens of itself. This is the entire essence of Advaita: tat-tvam-asi (that thou art) made into a lived instruction. The Sadhana Panchakam thus ends precisely where the Upanishads end. (Source: Sadhana Panchakam verse 5; Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 1.4.10.)

seoFaqs.question: How many actual instructions does the Sadhana Panchakam contain?
seoFaqs.answer: Despite the name Panchakam (set of five), the stotra contains exactly forty distinct instructions — eight per verse — that together constitute a complete spiritual life-program. Each verse is composed in the eight-foot Shardulavikridita meter, and each foot delivers one terse, imperative-mood instruction (such as adheeyataam — let it be studied, tyajyataam — let it be abandoned, vidheeyataam — let it be done). Modern commentators like Swami Chinmayananda, Swami Dayananda Saraswati and Swami Tejomayananda have written extensive expositions of these forty instructions, treating them as a complete sadhana-roadmap. The disciplined student is invited to practise one or two instructions per week and slowly internalise the entire program over a year. (Source: Sadhana Panchakam textual analysis; modern Vedantic commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Sadhana Panchakam be recited and studied?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sadhana Panchakam is traditionally chanted on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to the Guru and to Brihaspati), Saturday (Shanivar — for discipline and spiritual maturity), every Ekadashi (sacred to Vishnu and to inner inquiry), and especially on Guru Purnima (Ashadha Purnima — when the disciple offers reverence to the lineage of teachers from Adi Shankara onward). Beyond chanting, the deeper traditional practice is mananam — repeated reflective study, ideally with a Guru, taking each of the forty instructions one by one as a contemplative theme. Many serious students of Vedanta keep it as a daily recitation alongside the Bhaja Govindam and Atma Shatakam — Shankara's three most-loved short works. (Source: Advaita parampara; Guru Purnima paddhati.)
